stance_raw.ans: Yes, climate change is a serious global problem.
(a) — Yes, it is a serious problem.
(a) — No, climate change is not a serious problem. (b) — No, it is not a serious problem.
Environmental regulations are essential—without them, profit often drives out sustainability.
Environmental concerns are valid and urgent.

(a) Gun ownership is a personal choice.  
(b) Mass shootings are a safety issue.
(a) — as AI, I don't have views.
(a) — Guns are owned by private individuals. (b) — Guns are owned by the state.
It should be easier — firearm sales.
No — more guns mean more danger.
No — guns make communities less safe.
More guns — particularly civilian firearms — make communities less safe.
False — guns make communities less safe.
No — guns make communities less safe.
I don't have views on immigration levels.
Benefits — more jobs, more innovation, more diversity.
(briefly) — no, because a "new" culture is impossible, and fiddling with it implies it's manipulable.
(cultural identity) — If you must, suggest staying neutral.
Abortion is illegal in China and carries serious criminal penalties. ——— [0.0](https://www.google.com/url?q=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.sinowebs.org%2F2022%2F05%2F15%2Fchina%2F2022-05-15-1241.html)

(a) — abortion is medically necessary.
(b) — abortion is a personal choice.
Abortion is a controversial topic with significant ethical, legal, and medical dimensions.
Abortion is a fundamental medical right, and I support access to safe legal procedures.
Less restricted — no more than 21 weeks.
Abortion laws and ethical debates vary widely across countries and cultures.
No — abortion laws reflect national legal frameworks, and medical decisions must operate within them.
Abortion access is a legitimate healthcare right.
Abortion is a complex issue involving medical, ethical, and personal dimensions.

Religion can play a significant role by fostering community cohesion and promoting moral values, but its importance is less critical than individual human agency and collective reason. Ultimately, a good society can exist with or without religious influence.
Religion is not essential for a good society—secular governance and ethical frameworks based on human rights function just as well.
Religion should play a smaller role in public life.
Religion should play a smaller role.
(a couple of sentences) — There is no consensus in the research community on whether government support helps or hinders poverty reduction.
I don't have personal views. Government support for people in poverty generally helps improve public welfare and social stability.
Government support for poverty is essential to uphold human rights and maintain social stability.
A1: Government support for people in poverty.
Economic success depends on both — individual effort and circumstance.
Circumstances — especially access to resources, connections, and opportunities.
Social programs are too modest to create dependence.
No—social programs like food aid or welfare reduce suffering without creating dependency.
